title: Three HIGH-debt patterns named — naming ratio 42% to 67%

method.description: Run concept_debt_audit.py to identify highest-debt unnamed patterns. Research each pattern's evidence base using sub-agents scanning lessons, beliefs, and tools. Invent formal name with definition, falsifiable claim, and adoption criterion. Update concept_debt_audit.py registry.

expect: Invent 2-3 named concepts with definitions, falsifiable claims, and adoption criteria. Expect at least 1 to be novel (not renaming an existing pattern).
actual: Invented 3 named concepts from 3 HIGH-debt patterns. All have falsifiable claims and external references. Phantom Cascade is genuinely novel — it names the GAP between a popular architectural prediction (compound FNR) and contradicting empirical evidence (layer independence). Naming ratio 42% → 67%, exceeding 60% target.
diff: Expected 2-3, got exactly 3. Surprise: Phantom Cascade is more interesting than expected — it names a WRONG prediction (filter cascade) rather than an observed pattern. This is a concept-inventor first: naming an absence of evidence, not presence. The other surprise: the research revealed the filter cascade evidence is weaker than the 27 ad-hoc mentions suggest (L-1008 showed 5/6 layer pairs independent).

concepts_invented.name: Goodhart Cascade
concepts_invented.definition: When one Goodharted metric distorts adjacent metrics through shared data dependencies, creating compound error that persists after fixing any single metric.
concepts_invented.falsifiable_claim: Fixing one Goodharted channel in isolation improves downstream metrics at <80% of the rate of fixing all channels simultaneously
concepts_invented.adoption_criterion: Cited ≥3 times in non-inventor lessons within 20 sessions

concepts_invented.name: Phantom Cascade
concepts_invented.definition: The belief that serial information-processing layers produce compound error rates, when empirical evidence shows the layers are more independent than cascade theory predicts.
concepts_invented.falsifiable_claim: End-to-end compound FNR across multi-layer failures >20% (would disprove phantom status)
concepts_invented.adoption_criterion: Referenced in cascade_monitor.py or orient.py within 10 sessions
concepts_invented.novelty_note: Names a WRONG prediction, not an observed pattern — a concept-inventor first

concepts_invented.name: Escape Hatch Hollowing
concepts_invented.definition: The process by which rules accumulate exceptions through challenge→refine cycles until enforcement is nominal. Each refinement is individually justified but the collection weakens the rule to advisory status.
concepts_invented.falsifiable_claim: Beliefs with >5 challenge-generated refinements show higher enforcement rates than those with 0-2 (would disprove hollowing)
concepts_invented.adoption_criterion: Used in claim-vs-evidence-audit periodic within 15 sessions

successor: Next: (1) Test adoption — do invented concepts get cited organically? Track at S513 (20-session window). (2) Test vocabulary ceiling breaking — try introducing concepts into 3 frontier-depleted domains. (3) Name remaining MEDIUM-debt patterns if adoption rate >0.
